claim.hypotheses[0].explanation: Extracted H1 (revenue higher in UNIFORM) does not match the paper’s stated hypothesis about underpricing and potential collusion.

claim.hypotheses[1].explanation: Extracted H2 (less underpricing in UNIFORM) conflicts with the reference hypothesis that underpricing may be aggravated in UNIFORM due to collusion.

claim.hypotheses[2].explanation: Extracted H3 (no tacit collusion) contradicts the reference hypothesis suggesting collusion could aggravate underpricing.

claim.hypotheses_location.explanation: Reference places the hypothesis in Section 1: Introduction (p. 203). Extracted mentions Abstract and Introduction; partially correct but not precise.

claim.statement.explanation: Captures the core claim that UNIFORM raises more revenue than FIXED, but omits additional elements (higher prices and lower bidder payoffs).

claim.statement_location.explanation: Reference locates the statement in Section 4.1 (p. 212), whereas the extracted cites Abstract/Introduction.

data.source.explanation: Both state data come from the authors’ own laboratory experiment.

data.wave_or_subset.explanation: Reference lists NA; extracted supplies round/session details not specified in the reference.

data.sample_size.explanation: Reference: 112 participants (28 groups of 4). Extracted: 116 participants and 28 valid groups; group count matches but total N is incorrect.

data.unit_of_analysis.explanation: Reference unit is group; extracted lists multiple units (bids, bidders, rounds, group-level), diluting the specified unit.

data.notes.explanation: Reference notes one group excluded (originally 29). Extracted does not mention this but adds other experimental details.

method.description.explanation: Accurately describes experimental treatments and use of Wilcoxon tests; adds extra analyses (e.g., Spearman) not in the reference.

method.steps.explanation: Covers the key ordered steps similar to the reference, with extra analyses beyond the two-tailed Wilcoxon tests.

method.models.explanation: Includes the Wilcoxon signed-ranks test per reference, but adds additional methods (Spearman, theoretical comparisons).

method.outcome_variable.explanation: Includes average prices/market-clearing price (matches reference) but adds several additional outcomes not specified in the reference.

method.independent_variables.explanation: Includes treatment group (matches reference) plus additional factors (signals, bidder type, round) not noted in the reference.

method.control_variables.explanation: Reference lists NA; extracted notes no additional controls, consistent with NA.

method.tools_software.explanation: Reference specifies Visual Basic 6; extracted says not stated.

results.summary.explanation: Includes the main finding that UNIFORM has higher prices/revenues than FIXED, but adds several results not reported in the reference summary.
